name: Barthel Index of Activities of Daily Living
description: A widely used measure of functional independence in activities of daily living (ADL). Records actual functioning, not potential ability.
instructions: Choose the scoring point for the statement that most closely corresponds to the patient's current level of ability for each of the following 10 items. Record actual, not potential, functioning. Information can be obtained from patient self-report, a relative, or direct observation.

questions.question: Bowels
questions.description: Preceding week. If needs enema from nurse, score as incontinent.

options.label: Incontinent (or needs to be given enemas)

questions.question: Bladder
questions.description: Preceding week. A catheterized patient who can completely manage the catheter alone is 'continent'.

options.label: Incontinent, or catheterized and unable to manage

questions.question: Grooming
questions.description: Refers to personal hygiene: teeth, hair, shaving, washing face. Implements can be provided by helper.

questions.question: Toilet use
questions.description: Should be able to reach toilet/commode, undress sufficiently, clean self, dress, and leave.

options.label: Needs some help, but can do something alone

questions.question: Feeding
questions.description: Able to eat any normal food. Food cooked and served by others, but not cut up.

options.label: Needs help cutting, spreading butter, etc.

questions.question: Transfer (bed to chair and back)
questions.description: From bed to chair and back. Dependent = no sitting balance, two people to lift.

options.label: Major help (one or two people, physical), can sit

questions.question: Mobility
questions.description: Refers to mobility about house or ward, indoors. May use aid. If in wheelchair, must negotiate corners/doors unaided.

options.label: Walks with help of one person (verbal or physical)
Independent (but may use any aid, e.g., stick)

questions.question: Dressing
questions.description: Should be able to select and put on all clothes, which may be adapted.

options.label: Needs help, but can do about half unaided

questions.question: Stairs
questions.description: Must carry any walking aid used to be independent.

questions.question: Bathing
questions.description: Usually the most difficult activity. Must get in and out unsupervised and wash self.

severityBands.label: Independent
severityBands.description: Fully independent in all activities of daily living.

severityBands.label: Moderate Dependence
severityBands.description: Moderate assistance required for daily activities.

severityBands.label: Total Dependence
severityBands.description: Fully dependent; requires complete assistance for ADLs.

interpretation.scoringMethod: Sum all item scores. Range: 0-20. Higher scores indicate greater independence.
interpretation.changeThreshold: Changes of more than 2 points in total score reflect probable genuine change. Change on one item from fully dependent to independent is also likely reliable.
interpretation.clinicalNotes: The Index records what a patient actually does, not potential ability. Need for supervision renders the patient not independent. Middle categories imply the patient supplies over 50% of the effort. Use of aids is allowed for independence.

references.citation: Mahoney FI, Barthel DW. Functional evaluation: the Barthel Index. Md State Med J. 1965;14:61-65.
Collin C, Wade DT, Davies S, Horne V. The Barthel ADL Index: a reliability study. Int Disabil Stud. 1988;10(2):61-63.
Wade DT, Collin C. The Barthel ADL Index: a standard measure of physical disability? Int Disabil Stud. 1988;10(2):64-67.

metadata.author: Mahoney FI, Barthel DW (1965); Collin et al. (1988)
